'''Josh Smith''' (born 18 April 2001) is an Australian professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|wg}}er or {{rlp|ce}} for the [[Warrington Wolves]] in the [[Betfred Super League]].

==Background==
Smith was born in [[Echuca, Victoria]] and raised in [[Mackay, Queensland]], Australia.

He played for the [[Mackay & District Rugby League|Mackay Brothers]] as a junior.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.thechronicle.com.au/news/queensland/mackay/sport/young-trio-bolsters-cutters-isc-talent-pool/news-story/0e4f717b2857ad979b20a96dc7f5f82f|title=Young trio bolsters Cutters ISC talent pool}}</ref>

Smith has studied for a [[Bachelor of Secondary Education]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://risingcommunity.com.au/about-us/|title=About Us}}</ref>

==Early career==
Smith played for the [[Northern Marlins]] in the 2017 QRL State Championship.<ref name="auto">{{Cite web|url=https://18thman.com/players/josh-smith/|title=Josh Smith|date=November 2, 2019}}</ref>

He played for the [[Mackay Cutters]] at under 18 level in the 2019 [[Mal Meninga Cup]]. He played for their Colts side between 2019 and 2021.<ref name="auto"/>

==Career==
Smith played for the [[Mackay Cutters]] in the [[2021 Queensland Cup]], scoring 10 tries in 17 games.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.alloutrugbyleague.co.uk/news/warrington-wolves-next-hidden-gem-1594055|title=Warrington Wolves' secret signing aiming to be Super League's next hidden gem|first=Josh|last=McAllister|date=January 2, 2026|website=All Out Rugby League}}</ref> He was contracted to the [[North Queensland Cowboys]] at that time.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.cowboys.com.au/news/2021/09/23/eleven-cowboys-contracted-players-named-for-hdc-semis/|title=Eleven Cowboys contracted players named for HDC semis|date=September 22, 2021|website=North Queensland Cowboys}}</ref>

That form earn him a move to the [[Australian Capital Territory]] to play for the [[Canberra Raiders]] [[Jersey Flegg]] side for the 2022 season.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/players/nsw-cup/raiders/josh-smith/|title=Official The Knock On Effect NSW Cup profile of Josh Smith for Canberra Raiders NSW Cup | NRL.com|website=National Rugby League}}</ref>

Smith returned to [[Mackay Cutters|Mackay]] ahead of the [[2023 Queensland Cup]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.qrl.com.au/news/2023/03/07/mackay-cutters-cup-gains-and-losses-for-2023/|title=Mackay Cutters: Cup gains and losses for 2023|date=March 6, 2023|website=Queensland Rugby League}}</ref> He scored 22 tries in 40 games over the course of the 2023 and 2024 seasons for the [[Mackay Cutters|Cutters]].

He moved to the [[Brisbane Tigers]] ahead the [[2025 Queensland Cup]], scoring 10 tries in 20 games.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/clylq8wwp98o|title=Warrington Wolves sign James Bentley and Josh Smith ahead of 2026 season|date=October 30, 2025|website=BBC Sport}}</ref>

Smith moved to the [[Warrington Wolves]] ahead of the [[2026 Super League season]], signing a two-year deal at the [[Halliwell Jones Stadium]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/wolves/wolvesnews/25583842.warrington-wolves-confirm-james-bentley-josh-smith-deals/|title=Warrington Wolves confirm double signing to bolster edges|date=October 30, 2025|website=Warrington Guardian}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://warringtonwolves.com/news/2025/november/Josh-Smith-I-take-pride-in-my-yardage-carries-/|title=Warrington Wolves - Josh Smith: "I take pride in my yardage carries"|first=Warrington|last=Wolves|date=November 14, 2025|website=Warrington Wolves}}</ref> He made his début for the [[Warrington Wolves|Wire]] in the [[Challenge Cup]] against the [[Sheffield Eagles]] in February 2026.
